title: Apply for a new adult passport in Canada - Documents to support your identity - Canada.ca
content:
Apply for a new adult passport in Canada

## Documents to support your identity
If you’re applying for a new passport, you need to submit:
- at least 1 valid document to support your identity You can submit the original or a photocopy of both sides. Remember that each photocopy must be signed and dated by your: guarantor or signing official, if you used the statutory declaration instead of completing the guarantor section on the passport application form What’s a signing official? You use a signing official when you do not have a guarantor. The signing official completes a statutory declaration form instead of the guarantor section on the passport application form.
- You can submit the original or a photocopy of both sides.
- Remember that each photocopy must be signed and dated by your: guarantor or signing official, if you used the statutory declaration instead of completing the guarantor section on the passport application form What’s a signing official? You use a signing official when you do not have a guarantor. The signing official completes a statutory declaration form instead of the guarantor section on the passport application form.
- signing official, if you used the statutory declaration instead of completing the guarantor section on the passport application form What’s a signing official? You use a signing official when you do not have a guarantor. The signing official completes a statutory declaration form instead of the guarantor section on the passport application form.
You use a signing official when you do not have a guarantor. The signing official completes a statutory declaration form instead of the guarantor section on the passport application form.
- additional documents if you’re changing your name (if applicable)
If you’re renewing a passport, you don’t need to include a document to support your identity.
If your current passport is still valid, you must include it with your application. In some cases, you can use your Canadian passport as your identification document (ID) when applying for a new adult passport.
Tell us on your form if you need the visa and want your original passport back. If you don’t, the visa could be damaged when the previous passport is cancelled.
- Requirements for your ID
- Types of ID you can use
- Using a Canadian passport as your ID
- How to submit your ID

## Requirements for your ID
To support your identity, your ID must:
- include your name date of birth photo signature
- be issued by a federal, provincial, territorial or state government authority, or the equivalent abroad
If you’re applying by mail, we recommend sending photocopies of your ID. If you send photocopies:
- copy both sides of the ID
- have your guarantor or signing official sign them Learn how to submit your documents .
- Learn how to submit your documents .

### If your ID doesn’t have all the required information
You can include more than 1 ID if all of the required information isn’t on the same document.
To prove that each document belongs to you, they must share at least 1 piece of information, such as your name. This is so we can be sure they all belong to you.
We may accept it if there isn’t normally a signature on that particular type of ID.
If it was issued to you without one, you can leave this field blank on the application form.
You use a signing official when you do not have a guarantor. The signing official completes a Statutory Declaration instead of a Guarantor form.

## Types of ID you can use
You can use any of the following types of ID alone or in combination to meet the above requirements :
- driver’s licence issued by a province or territory, or the equivalent abroad
- health card issued by a province or territory, or the equivalent abroad
- Certificate of Indian Status
- non-driver’s licence issued by a province or territory, or the equivalent abroad
- ID card issued by the federal government, or a provincial or territorial government, or the equivalent abroad
- employee identification issued by the federal government, or a provincial or territorial government, or the equivalent abroad
You can’t use your social insurance number (SIN) card as ID.
If your ID isn’t in English or French, you must provide an official translation of it.

## Using a Canadian passport as your ID
You may be able to use your previous passport as your ID.

### You applied for your previous passport when you were 16 or older
You can use your previous passport as ID if
- it was valid for 5 years or more when you got it
- it’s either still valid or it’s been expired for 1 year or less
You can’t use your previous passport as ID if it
- has been expired for more than 1 year
- was a limited-validity passport of 2 years or less
- was reported lost or stolen

#### If your passport’s been expired for 1 year or less
You can send us your original passport or a photocopy of pages 2 and 3.
If you send us photocopies, you must have them signed and dated by your guarantor or signing official .

### You applied for your previous passport when you were 15 or younger
You can use your previous passport as ID if:
- it was valid for 5 years or more when you got it
- it’s still valid or it has been expired for only 1 year or less and
- you submit another piece of ID that’s government-issued valid has a common element, such as your name, to link your IDs
- has a common element, such as your name, to link your IDs
You can’t use your previous passport as ID if it:
- has been expired for more than 1 year
- was a limited-validity passport of 2 years or less
- was reported lost or stolen

## How to submit your ID
Whether you apply by mail or in person, you can submit either original documents or photocopies with your application.

### Applying in person (passport applications only)
- We’ll check your ID when you submit your application.
- We’ll return it to you before you leave.
- We recommend you submit photocopies.
- This will ensure you can use the original documents while we process your application.

### Using photocopies as your ID
- copy both sides of the document
- have your guarantor or signing official sign and date each side of your photocopies

### Using original documents as your ID
We’ll return them to you by mail. They may be sent in separate envelopes.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
